content.text:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Commands
6	
7	```bash
8	# Start all services (one-click)
9	docker compose up
10	
11	# Run all tests (unit + API integration)
12	./run_tests.sh
13	
14	# Unit tests only (no services needed) — 244 tests
15	python3 -m unittest discover -s unit_tests -p "test_*.py" -v
16	
17	# Single unit test file
18	python3 -m unittest unit_tests.backend.test_password -v
19	
20	# API integration tests (requires running backend on :8000)
21	API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000 python3 -m unittest discover -s API_tests -p "test_*.py" -v
22	
23	# Backend build (inside backend/)
24	cargo build --release
25	
26	# Frontend WASM build (inside frontend/, requires trunk and wasm32-unknown-unknown target)
27	trunk build --release
28	```
29	
30	## Architecture
31	
32	Three-tier monorepo: **Dioxus/WASM frontend** (nginx :3000) → **Rocket API** (:8000) → **MySQL 8.0** (:3306).
33	
34	### Backend (Rust + Rocket)
35	
36	Layered architecture with strict dependency flow:
37	
38	**routes/** (HTTP handlers, validation) → **services/** (business logic) → **repositories/** (SQL queries) → **models/** (entities)
39	
40	Supporting modules:
41	- `middleware/auth_guard.rs` — JWT extraction from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header; guards: `AdminGuard`, `StaffAuthorGuard`, `DeptReviewerGuard`, `CourseAuthorGuard`, `ReviewerGuard`. Admin role bypasses all role checks.
42	- `middleware/rate_limiter.rs` — 120 req/min per user via DB-backed sliding window
43	- `middleware/reauth_guard.rs` — `ReauthRequired` guard enforces re-auth within 15 min for admin actions
44	- `middleware/hmac_guard.rs` — HMAC-SHA256 signature verification with nonce anti-replay (5 min expiry)
45	- `middleware/correlation.rs` — `X-Correlation-Id` fairing, auto-generates UUID if not present
46	- `middleware/csrf_guard.rs` — Origin header check fairing for state-changing requests (defense-in-depth; primary CSRF defense is Bearer token auth)
47	- `auth/` — JWT (HS256), bcrypt passwords, HMAC signing utilities
48	- `dto/` — request/response schemas per domain (auth, course, approval, booking, term)
49	- `services/` — business logic: auth_service, course_service, approval_service, version_service, audit_service, term_service, booking_service
50	- `jobs/` — background tasks: scheduled transitions processor, expired data cleanup
51	
52	JWT Claims: `sub` (uuid), `user_id` (i64), `username`, `role`, `department_id`, `exp`, `iat`
53	
54	All routes mounted under `/api/v1` except `/health`.
55	
56	### Frontend (Dioxus 0.6)
57	
58	- `auth/mod.rs` — `AUTH: GlobalSignal<AuthState>` is the single source of auth truth
59	- `api/mod.rs` — HTTP client auto-attaches Bearer token from LocalStorage (`campus_learn_token`)
60	- `layouts/mod.rs` — `MainLayout` renders role-aware sidebar navigation
61	- nginx config in `frontend/Dockerfile` proxies `/api/` and `/health` to `backend:8000`
62	
63	### Database
64	
65	- SQLx migrations in `backend/migrations/` auto-run at startup via `sqlx::migrate!()`
66	- Migration naming: `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description.sql`
67	- Schema: 30+ tables across domains (core, auth, academic, versioning, approvals, bookings, compliance, audit, notifications, privacy)
68	- Seeding (`services/seed.rs`): creates departments, permissions, and 5 default accounts on first boot
69	
70	## Conventions
71	
72	### API Response Format
73	
74	Success: `{ "success": true, "data": T, "message": null }`
75	Error: `{ "status": 400, "error": "Bad Request", "message": "...", "details": null }`
76	
77	### Roles
78	
79	`admin` > `staff_author` > `dept_reviewer` > `faculty` > `student` (+ `integration` for API keys) — MySQL ENUM.
80	
81	### Course Status Flow
82	
83	`draft` → `pending_approval` → (step1: dept_reviewer, step2: admin) → `approved_scheduled` or `published` → `unpublished`
84	Rejection returns to `rejected` (re-editable). Author cannot self-approve.
85	
86	### Booking Status Flow
87	
88	- If `resource.requires_approval == false`: booking created with `status='confirmed'` immediately.
89	- If `resource.requires_approval == true`: booking created with `status='pending'`, requires reviewer/admin approval.
90	- `POST /api/v1/bookings/<uuid>/approve` (ReviewerGuard) — transitions pending → confirmed.
91	- `POST /api/v1/bookings/<uuid>/reject` (ReviewerGuard) — transitions pending → cancelled.
92	- Late cancellation breach only applies to confirmed bookings (not pending).
93	
94	### Terms Acceptance
95	
96	- `POST /api/v1/terms/<term_uuid>/accept` — idempotent acceptance of a term.
97	- `GET /api/v1/terms/my-acceptances` — list user's term acceptances.
98	- Enforcement: active term acceptance required before booking creation and course approval submission.
99	
100	### Media Pipeline
101	
102	- `POST /api/v1/courses/media/upload` — multipart file upload (PDF/MP4/PNG, max 500MB). Stores file locally, creates record with `status='pending_scan'`.
103	- `POST /api/v1/courses/media` — metadata-only registration (file stored externally). Also creates with `status='pending_scan'`.
104	- `POST /api/v1/courses/media/<uuid>/validate` — deterministic validation (extension↔MIME match, checksum, absolute path). Transitions to `ready` or `failed`.
105	- Course approval submission blocked if any media assets are not in `ready` status.
106	
107	### Default Seeded Accounts
108	
109	| Username | Password | Role |
110	|----------|----------|------|
111	| admin | Admin@12345678 | admin |
112	| author | Author@1234567 | staff_author |
113	| reviewer | Review@1234567 | dept_reviewer |
114	| faculty | Faculty@123456 | faculty |
115	| student | Student@12345 | student |
116	
117	## Non-Obvious Details
118	
119	- Backend Docker build uses `SQLX_OFFLINE=true` — runtime queries use string-based `sqlx::query_as` (not `query_as!` macro)
120	- Frontend served by nginx which reverse-proxies API calls to backend container
121	- `docker-compose.yml` uses `service_healthy` conditions for startup ordering
122	- Tests are Python (unittest), not Rust — unit tests validate business rules; API tests hit the live backend
123	- Audit logs have 7-year retention (`retention_expires_at` column, set via `DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 YEAR)`)
124	- Version snapshots retained for 180 days (configurable via `VERSION_RETENTION_DAYS`)
125	- Scheduled transitions processed via `POST /api/v1/approvals/process-scheduled` (admin-only, call from cron/background)
126

You are a senior full-stack engineer working in this repository:

- Backend: Rust (Rocket), MySQL, SQL migrations
- Frontend: Dioxus (Rust/WASM)
- Tests: Python API tests + unit tests
- Constraints: offline-first internal portal, local integrations only, strict RBAC + privacy/security compliance

Your mission is to remediate all High and Medium issues listed in `.tmp/delivery_acceptance_architecture_audit.md` and make the project acceptable for delivery.

## Non-negotiable engineering requirements

1) Keep architecture clean (routes/services/repositories/models separation).
2) Prefer minimal-risk incremental changes with backward compatibility where possible.
3) Add/adjust migrations for schema-level fixes.
4) Add/adjust backend and frontend tests for every fixed behavior.
5) Update docs (`README.md`, `SELF_TEST_REPORT.md`) so claims match reality.
6) Run quality gates before finishing:
   - backend build
   - frontend build
   - API tests (targeted + relevant full suite)
   - unit tests
7) Provide a final “requirements coverage” checklist mapping each audit issue -> fixed evidence (files + tests).

---

## Required fixes (implement all)

### A. Privacy workflows (HIGH)
- Replace placeholder implementations for export/delete/rectify:
  - `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs`
  - `backend/src/repositories/privacy_repo.rs`
  - related routes/dto/models as needed
- Export must generate real export artifact metadata and content flow suitable for offline use.
- Delete must enforce full-account deletion/retention policy (not just vault row delete).
- Rectify must perform actual field-level updates with audit trail.
- Preserve admin approval workflow and log all actions in audit/security logs.

### B. Risk subscriptions + webhook delivery integration (HIGH)
- Wire risk event creation to subscription matching and webhook queueing:
  - `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs`
  - `backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs`
  - related repos/models/routes
- Replace hard-coded role fan-out with subscription-driven delivery + scoped notifications.
- Ensure retry/queued-local behavior when endpoint unreachable.
- Add tests validating event -> subscription match -> queued delivery.

### C. Booking approval authorization scope + breach visibility (HIGH)
- Add department/ownership scope for booking approval decisions (schema + service enforcement).
- Ensure reviewer can only approve/reject in allowed scope.
- Expose breach visibility for approvers in booking decision flow.
- Touch likely files:
  - migrations under `backend/migrations/`
  - `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs`
  - `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs`
  - `backend/src/repositories/booking_repo.rs`
- Add tests for cross-department denial and in-scope approval success.

### D. Rate limiting for unauthenticated entry points (HIGH)
- Add robust throttling for login and other high-risk unauthenticated endpoints.
- Include IP-based + account-aware protections for `/auth/login`.
- Ensure anti-scraping protections are applied pre-auth where relevant.
- Add tests in `API_tests/test_rate_limit.py` and auth tests for brute-force patterns.

### E. Consistent re-auth enforcement for admin actions (HIGH)
- Define explicit admin-sensitive action set and enforce `ReauthRequired` consistently.
- Must include approvals decisions, booking approve/reject, and audit access (and any equivalent sensitive mutations/reads).
- Update route guards and tests:
  - `API_tests/test_approvals.py`
  - `API_tests/test_booking_approval.py`
  - `API_tests/test_audit.py`
  - `API_tests/test_security_regression.py`

### F. Frontend missing critical flows (HIGH)
Implement missing UI/UX in Dioxus:

1. Course release workflows:
   - media upload/validation flow
   - tag management flow
   - unpublish flow
   - version diff presentation between approved releases
2. Booking workflows:
   - reviewer approval queue
   - conflict/availability check during booking creation (immediate feedback)
3. Responsive nav fix:
   - mobile alternative navigation when sidebar hidden

Likely files:
- `frontend/src/pages/course_editor.rs`
- `frontend/src/pages/course_detail.rs`
- `frontend/src/pages/bookings.rs`
- `frontend/src/api/mod.rs`
- `frontend/src/layouts/mod.rs`
- `frontend/assets/main.css`

### G. Department/term isolation in notifications (HIGH)
- Remove broad role-wide notification fan-out.
- Implement recipient resolution scoped to department/term/entity ownership.
- Update:
  - `backend/src/services/notification_service.rs`
  - relevant repos/services invoking notifications
- Add security regression tests for metadata isolation leaks.

### H. Documentation/test-report accuracy (MEDIUM)
- Align claims with actual implementation:
  - `README.md`
  - `SELF_TEST_REPORT.md`
- Remove/qualify any unverifiable runtime assertions.
- Fix route/protection mismatches in docs.

### I. API response envelope consistency (MEDIUM)
- Standardize success responses on documented envelope OR explicitly document exceptions and enforce them consistently.
- Cover routes including `/auth/me`, `/info`, `/health`.
- Update tests to validate real handler output (not static mock dict checks only).

### J. HMAC integration operability and verification path (MEDIUM)
- Add practical key provisioning/rotation path and documentation for local static verification.
- Ensure scheduled/internal endpoints can be tested/verifiable end-to-end offline.
- Add API tests for:
  - valid signature
  - replay rejection
  - timestamp expiry
  - scheduled transition execution

### K. Improve test strategy quality (MEDIUM/HIGH)
- Reduce reliance on Python mirror tests for Rust behavior.
- Add real Rust unit/integration tests for critical backend logic where feasible.
- Keep Python API tests for black-box behavior; remove/label mirror-only tests where misleading.
- Ensure critical paths are tested:
  - privacy semantics
  - webhook queueing
  - booking scope auth
  - re-auth matrix
  - response envelope
  - HMAC flow
  - login throttling
  - proxy/HTTPS path (where feasible in current setup)

---

## Implementation approach constraints

- Make small coherent commits per domain (security/privacy/booking/frontend/docs/tests).
- Do not introduce cloud dependencies; keep offline-compatible.
- Preserve existing coding style and module boundaries.
- Add/modify migrations safely and ensure idempotent upgrade path.

---

## Final output format required from you

1) Summary of changes by issue ID/title.
2) Exact files changed with one-line purpose.
3) Migration notes (new SQL migrations and why).
4) Tests added/updated and what each verifies.
5) Quality gates result:
   - Build: PASS/FAIL
   - Lint/type checks: PASS/FAIL
   - Unit tests: PASS/FAIL
   - API tests: PASS/FAIL
6) Requirements coverage table mapping each audit issue to:
   - Status: Fixed / Partial / Deferred
   - Evidence: file paths + test names
   - If Deferred: clear blocker and next action.

text.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the code I've examined.

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

I have thoroughly explored all the key areas you requested. Here's the complete analysis:

### 1. PRIVACY WORKFLOWS

**Files Location:**
- `/backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs` - Service layer with business logic
- `/backend/src/repositories/privacy_repo.rs` - Data access layer
- `/backend/src/routes/privacy.rs` - API endpoints
- `/backend/src/dto/privacy.rs` - Data transfer objects
- `/backend/src/models/privacy.rs` - Domain models

**Key Functions:**

**Privacy Service (privacy_service.rs):**
- `create_data_request(pool, user_id, req)` - Creates GDPR-style data requests (export/delete/rectify types). Validates request type, generates UUID, stores in DB, creates audit log
- `admin_review_request(pool, request_uuid, req, admin_id)` - Admin approval/rejection workflow. Handles three types: export (generates file path), delete (calls delete_user_sensitive_data), rectify (no-op)
- `list_requests(pool, status)` - Lists all data requests, optionally filtered by status
- `list_user_requests(pool, user_id)` - Lists requests for a specific user
- `store_sensitive_field(pool, user_id, field_name, value, encryption_key, key_version)` - Encrypts and stores sensitive data in vault. key_version=2 identifies records using dedicated DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY
- `get_masked_fields(pool, user_id)` - Returns masked sensitive fields (SSN as ***-**-****, bank_account as ****####, etc.)

**Privacy Repository (privacy_repo.rs):**
- `create_data_request()` - SQL INSERT
- `find_data_request_by_uuid()` - Fetches single request
- `list_data_requests(status?)` - Fetches all or filtered
- `list_user_data_requests(user_id)` - User-specific list
- `approve_data_request()` - Sets status='processing', stores approved_by/admin_notes
- `reject_data_request()` - Sets status='rejected'
- `complete_data_request()` - Sets status='completed', stores result_file_path
- `store_encrypted()` - Inserts/updates sensitive_data_vault with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
- `get_encrypted()` - Fetches encrypted field by user_id + field_name
- `list_encrypted_fields()` - All encrypted fields for a user
- `delete_user_sensitive_data()` - Purges all vault entries for a user

**Privacy Routes (privacy.rs):**
- `POST /requests` - Create data request (authenticated user)
- `GET /requests?status=...` - List all requests (admin only)
- `GET /requests/my` - User's own requests
- `POST /requests/<uuid>/review` - Admin approval/rejection (requires reauthentication)
- `POST /sensitive` - Store encrypted field (uses config.data_encryption_key, key_version=2)
- `GET /sensitive` - Retrieve masked fields (no decryption, masking only)

**Models (privacy.rs):**
- `PersonalDataRequest` - id, uuid, user_id, request_type (export/delete/rectify), status (pending/processing/completed/rejected), reason, processed_by, processed_at, approved_by, approved_at, admin_notes, result_file_path, timestamps
- `SensitiveDataVault` - id, uuid, user_id, field_name, encrypted_value, iv, key_version (1=legacy SHA256, 2=dedicated key), timestamps

**DTOs (privacy.rs):**
- `CreateDataRequest` - request_type, reason
- `AdminReviewDataRequest` - approved (bool), admin_notes
- `DataRequestResponse` - Full response with all fields
- `StoreSensitiveDataRequest` - field_name, value
- `MaskedFieldResponse` - field_name, masked_value

---

### 2. RISK + WEBHOOK WORKFLOWS

**Files Location:**
- `/backend/src/services/risk_service.rs` - Risk evaluation engine
- `/backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs` - Webhook validation, queuing, delivery
- `/backend/src/repositories/risk_repo.rs` - Risk data access
- `/backend/src/models/risk.rs` - Risk domain models
- `/backend/src/dto/risk.rs` - Risk DTOs
- `/backend/src/routes/risk.rs` - Risk API endpoints
- `/backend/src/repositories/webhook_repo.rs` - Webhook data access

**Risk Service Functions (risk_service.rs):**
- `list_rules(pool)` - Fetches all active risk rules with last_run_at formatting
- `list_events(pool, limit?)` - Fetches risk events (default limit=100), joins with rule names
- `update_event(pool, event_uuid, req, reviewer_id)` - Updates event status (acknowledged/mitigated/false_positive/escalated). Validates status enum, creates audit log
- `run_risk_evaluation(pool)` - Scheduled job runner. Fetches due rules, dispatches to evaluation functions by type, updates last_run_at
  - Evaluation types: posting_frequency, blacklisted_employer, abnormal_compensation, duplicate_posting
- `evaluate_posting_frequency()` - Detects employers posting >max_postings times in window_hours. Creates risk events, notifies admins
- `evaluate_blacklisted_employers()` - Finds postings from active blacklisted employers in last 24h. Flags posting, creates security event, creates risk event
- `evaluate_abnormal_compensation()` - Detects adjunct postings with compensation <min_amount or >max_amount. Score 80 (too low), 90 (too high)
- `evaluate_duplicate_postings()` - Detects employer+title combinations posted >1 time in window_hours. Risk score 70
- `create_subscription(pool, user_id, event_type, channel?, target_url?, signing_secret?)` - Creates webhook or in_app subscription. Validates webhook URLs
- `list_subscriptions(pool, user_id)` - User's subscriptions
- `create_posting(pool, req, user_id)` - Creates employer posting. Checks blacklist first, errors if blacklisted
- `add_blacklist(pool, req, user_id)` - Adds employer to blacklist

**Webhook Service Functions (webhook_service.rs):**
- `validate_webhook_endpoint(url)` - Validates on-prem webhook URLs:
  - Scheme: http or https only
  - Host: localhost, 127.0.0.1, 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, 192.168.x.x, or bare hostname (alphanumeric+hyphens, no dots)
  - Rejects IPv6, cloud endpoints, internet-facing hosts
- `enqueue_event_webhooks(pool, event_type, payload)` - For all active webhook subscribers to event_type:
  - Signs payload with per-subscription secret (if configured) using HMAC-SHA256
  - Inserts into webhook_queue with signature, is_onprem=true, next_attempt_at=NOW()
- `process_webhook_queue(pool)` - Processes up to 50 pending webhooks:
  - Calls attempt_delivery() (simplified HTTP POST with 10s timeout)
  - On 2xx-3xx: mark_delivered with response_code
  - On error: mark_failed with exponential backoff (2^attempts * 30 seconds)
  - If max_attempts exceeded: creates dead-letter security event
  - Returns (delivered_count, failed_count)
- `attempt_delivery()` - Makes HTTP POST with Content-Type: application/json, optional X-Webhook-Signature header

**Risk Repository Functions (risk_repo.rs):**
- `list_rules()` - SELECT all WHERE is_active=true ORDER BY name
- `find_rule_by_id(id)` - Fetch single rule
- `get_rules_due_for_run()` - SELECT WHERE is_active=true AND (last_run_at IS NULL OR last_run_at < NOW() - interval minutes)
- `update_rule_last_run(id)` - SET last_run_at=NOW(), updated_at=NOW()
- `create_risk_event()` - INSERT risk_event with status='new'. Details stored as JSON string
- `list_risk_events(limit)` - ORDER BY created_at DESC
- `find_risk_event_by_uuid(uuid)` - Fetch single event
- `update_risk_event_status()` - Updates status, reviewed_by, reviewed_at (IF reviewed_by IS NOT NULL), notes (COALESCE), escalated_to
- `list_blacklisted_employers()` - All active blacklist entries
- `is_employer_blacklisted()` - Boolean check
- `add_blacklisted_employer()` - INSERT with is_active=true default
- `create_posting()` - INSERT employer posting
- `count_postings_in_window()` - COUNT WHERE employer_name AND created_at > NOW() - interval hours
- `find_duplicate_postings()` - COUNT WHERE employer_name AND title AND created_at > NOW() - interval hours
- `create_subscription()` - INSERT IGNORE (handles duplicates gracefully)
- `list_subscriptions(user_id)` - All active subscriptions for user
- `get_subscribers_for_event(event_type)` - All active subscribers to event_type
- `delete_subscription(id)` - SET is_active=false

**Webhook Repository Functions (webhook_repo.rs):**
- `enqueue_webhook()` - INSERT webhook_queue with status='pending', is_onprem=TRUE, next_attempt_at=NOW()
- `get_pending_webhooks(limit)` - SELECT WHERE status='pending' AND next_attempt_at <= NOW() AND attempts < max_attempts, ORDER BY created_at, LIMIT
- `mark_delivered()` - SET status='delivered', response_code, last_attempt_at=NOW(), attempts+1
- `mark_failed()` - SET status=(IF attempts+1>=max_attempts THEN 'dead_letter' ELSE 'pending'), response_code, response_body, last_attempt_at=NOW(), attempts+1, next_attempt_at=DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL POW(2, attempts)*30 SECOND)
- `list_webhooks(limit)` - ORDER BY created_at DESC

**Risk Routes (risk.rs):**
- `GET /rules` - List rules (admin only)
- `GET /events?limit=...` - List events (admin only)

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHTS

1. **Privacy Architecture:**
   - Dual-track: personal_data_requests (GDPR workflow) + sensitive_data_vault (encrypted storage)
   - key_version field enables encryption key rotation (1=legacy, 2=current dedicated key)
   - Masking rules defined in service layer (mask_value function)
   - Admin approval required for all request types

2. **Risk Engine:**
   - Scheduled evaluation by rule type (posting_frequency, blacklisted_employer, abnormal_compensation, duplicate_postings)
   - Risk scoring: 70-100 scale
   - Integration with security_repo for critical events (blacklisted employer detection)
   - Escalation chain: risk_events → escalated_to user

3. **Webhook System:**
   - On-prem only validation: blocks internet endpoints, allows private IPs
   - Per-subscription signing secrets with HMAC-SHA256
   - Exponential backoff: next_attempt = NOW() + (2^attempts * 30 seconds)
   - Dead-lettering: events discarded after max_attempts with security event logged
   - Separate payload storage (JSON in webhook_queue)

4. **Notification System:**
   - Simple in-app notification model (no email/SMS yet, though schema supports channels)
   - Role-based broadcasting (notify_role fetches all users with role)
   - Unread count optimization with index (user_id, is_read)
   - Entity linking: entity_type + entity_uuid for navigation

5. **Encryption & Key Management:**
   - DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY used for AES-256-GCM encryption
   - IV stored alongside ciphertext
   - key_version=2 marks records using dedicated key (for rotation)
   - Legacy key_version=1 stored for backward compatibility

6. **Approval Workflows:**
   - Two-step approval (pending_step1 → pending_step2 → approved/rejected)
   - Scheduled transitions: courses can transition at future datetime (approved_scheduled → published)
   - Status enum: draft → pending_approval → approved_scheduled → published → pending_unpublish → unpublished

7. **Audit & Compliance:**
   - All privacy/risk/approval actions logged to audit_logs with action/entity/user tracking
   - Security events for critical actions (blacklisted posting attempts, webhook dead-letters)
   - Correlation IDs for tracing related events
   - Retention expiry dates for log cleanup

All SQL queries use parameterized statements with sqlx::query bindings, providing full SQL injection protection.

- `Resource` - id, uuid, name, resource_type, location, capacity, description, open_time (NaiveTime), close_time (NaiveTime), max_booking_hours, requires_approval, is_active, timestamps
- `Booking` - id, uuid, resource_id, booked_by, title, description, start_time, end_time, status (pending/confirmed/cancelled), recurrence_rule, reschedule_count, approved_by, approved_at, timestamps

#### B. DTOs (`backend/src/dto/booking.rs`)
- `ResourceResponse` - returned resource info with times as strings

- `BookingResponse` - full booking with status, reschedule_count, approval info
- `RescheduleRequest` - new times and optional reason
- `BreachResponse` - breach info
- `RestrictionResponse` - restriction with is_active and auto_triggered flags

#### C. Booking Repository (`backend/src/repositories/booking_repo.rs`)

**Key SQL Queries:**

1. **Atomic Booking Creation** (`create_booking_atomic`):
   ```sql
   SELECT id FROM resources WHERE id = ? FOR UPDATE
   SELECT id FROM bookings WHERE resource_id = ? AND status IN ('confirmed', 'pending') AND start_time < ? AND end_time > ? LIMIT 1
   SELECT id FROM resource_blackouts WHERE resource_id = ? AND start_time < ? AND end_time > ? LIMIT 1
   INSERT INTO bookings (uuid, resource_id, booked_by, title, description, start_time, end_time, status) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   ```
   - Uses `SELECT FOR UPDATE` for row-level locking
   - Returns error "BOOKING_CONFLICT" or "BLACKOUT_CONFLICT" on conflict

2. **Reschedule Atomic** (`reschedule_booking_atomic`):
   - Locks resource, checks conflicts excluding the booking being rescheduled
   - Records reschedule history in `booking_reschedules` table
   - Updates booking times and increments `reschedule_count`

3. **Approval/Rejection**:
   ```sql
   UPDATE bookings SET status = 'confirmed', approved_by = ?, approved_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = ? AND status = 'pending'
   UPDATE bookings SET status = 'cancelled', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = ? AND status = 'pending'
   ```

4. **Restriction Checks**:
   ```sql
   SELECT id FROM restrictions WHERE user_id = ? AND restriction_type = ? AND is_active = TRUE AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > NOW()) LIMIT 1
   ```

5. **Breach Management**:
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO breaches (uuid, user_id, booking_id, breach_type, severity, description, status) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'open')
   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM breaches WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at > DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ? DAY)
   ```

#### D. Booking Service (`backend/src/services/booking_service.rs`)

**Major Functions:**

1. **`list_resources()`** - Returns active resources with times formatted as HH:MM strings

2. **`check_availability()`** - Returns hourly slots for a date
   - Generates slots from open_time to close_time
   - Marks unavailable if: blackout overlap or existing booking overlap
   - Returns `AvailabilitySlot` with reason

3. **`create_booking()`**
   - Validates: user doesn't have "booking_suspended" restriction
   - Enforces term acceptance via `term_service::check_active_term_accepted()`
   - Validates: end > start, within 90 days, within operating hours, within max duration, max 2 active per resource
   - Determines status: "pending" if `requires_approval`, else "confirmed"
   - Calls atomic create with conflict checking
   - On success: audits via `audit_repo` and notifies user + dept_reviewer if pending
   - Errors map "BOOKING_CONFLICT" → validation error, "BLACKOUT_CONFLICT" → validation error

4. **`reschedule_booking()`**
   - Checks: user owns booking, status is "confirmed", reschedule_count < MAX_RESCHEDULES (2)
   - Validates new times: end > start, within hours, within max duration
   - Calls atomic reschedule
   - Maps conflict error

5. **`cancel_booking()`**
   - Checks: user owns booking OR is admin
   - Updates status to "cancelled"
   - **Late Cancellation Breach Logic**:
     - If confirmed AND hours_until_start < LATE_CANCEL_HOURS (2) AND start > now:
       - Creates breach with type "late_cancellation" and severity "medium"
       - Notifies user
       - Counts recent breaches (last BREACH_WINDOW_DAYS=60 days)
       - If breach_count >= BREACH_THRESHOLD (3): auto-creates 30-day "booking_suspended" restriction
       - Logs security event "booking_auto_restriction"

6. **`approve_booking()`** - Updates status to "confirmed", sets approved_by/approved_at
   - Only for "pending" bookings
   - Notifies user

7. **`reject_booking()`** - Sets status to "cancelled"
   - Only for "pending" bookings
   - Logs reason
   - Notifies user

8. **`list_user_bookings()`** - Returns all bookings for user with resource names

9. **`list_breaches()`** - Returns user's breaches

10. **`list_restrictions()`** - Returns active restrictions for user

### 2. AUTH + RATE LIMITING

#### A. AuthGuard (`backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs`)

**`AuthenticatedUser` Guard:**
1. Extracts "Authorization: Bearer <token>" header
2. Validates token via `validate_token()` from `crate::auth::jwt`
3. **Rate Limiting Applied Here** (inline in `from_request`):
   ```sql
   SELECT request_count FROM rate_limit_entries WHERE user_id = ? AND window_start > DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE) ORDER BY window_start DESC LIMIT 1
   UPDATE rate_limit_entries SET request_count = request_count + 1 WHERE user_id = ? AND window_start > DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
   -- If no rows updated: INSERT INTO rate_limit_entries (user_id, window_start, request_count) VALUES (?, NOW(), 1)
   ```
   - Limit: `config.rate_limit_per_minute` (default 120)
   - Returns 429 if exceeded with message "Rate limit exceeded. Try again in a minute."
4. Sets `Outcome::Success(AuthenticatedUser { claims })`

**Role-Based Guards** (macro-generated):
- `AdminGuard` - requires role == "admin"
- `StaffAuthorGuard` - requires role == "staff_author" or "admin"
- `DeptReviewerGuard` - requires role == "dept_reviewer" or "admin"
- `FacultyGuard` - requires role == "faculty" or "admin"
- `CourseAuthorGuard` - requires role in ["admin", "staff_author"]
- `ReviewerGuard` - requires role in ["admin", "dept_reviewer"]

All delegates to `AuthenticatedUser::from_request()` first, so rate limiting applies to all.

#### B. Rate Limiter (`backend/src/middleware/rate_limiter.rs`)

**`RateLimited` Guard:**
- Simple wrapper that delegates entirely to `AuthenticatedUser`
- Exists for explicit call-site documentation
- No additional increment or check

#### C. ReAuth Guard (`backend/src/middleware/reauth_guard.rs`)

**`ReauthRequired` Guard:**
1. Delegates to `AuthenticatedUser` first
2. Queries `users` table: `SELECT ... FROM users WHERE id = ?`
3. Checks `last_reauth_at` timestamp:
   - Requires re-auth if: `(NOW() - last_reauth_at).num_minutes() >= config.reauth_window_minutes`
   - Default window: 15 minutes
4. Returns 403 "Re-authentication required for this action. POST /api/v1/auth/reauth first." if expired

#### D. HMAC Guard (`backend/src/middleware/hmac_guard.rs`)

**`HmacVerified` Guard:**
1. Extracts headers:
   - `X-HMAC-Key-Id` - API key identifier
   - `X-HMAC-Nonce` - one-time value
   - `X-HMAC-Timestamp` - request timestamp (i64)
   - `X-HMAC-Signature` - signature to verify

2. Validates timestamp freshness:
   ```rust
   !hmac::is_timestamp_valid(timestamp, config.hmac_nonce_expiry_seconds)
   ```
   - Default expiry: 300 seconds (5 minutes)

3. Prevents replay attacks:
   ```sql
   SELECT id FROM used_nonces WHERE nonce = ?
   -- If found: return 401 "Nonce already used (replay attack prevented)"
   ```

4. Looks up HMAC key:
   ```sql
   SELECT secret_hash FROM hmac_keys WHERE key_id = ? AND is_active = true AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > NOW())
   ```

5. Builds message: `hmac::build_signing_message(&key_id, &nonce, timestamp, method, path)`
   - Signs METHOD + PATH, not client-controlled headers

6. Verifies signature: `hmac::verify_signature(&secret, &message, &signature)`

7. Stores nonce to prevent replay:
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO used_nonces (nonce, key_id, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL 5 MINUTE))
   ```

#### E. Auth Routes (`backend/src/routes/auth.rs`)

**Endpoints:**

| Endpoint | Method | Guard | Handler |
|----------|--------|-------|---------|
| `/login` | POST | None | `login()` - validates LoginRequest, calls auth_service |
| `/change-password` | POST | ReauthRequired | `change_password()` - validates ChangePasswordRequest |
| `/reauth` | POST | AuthenticatedUser | `reauth()` - validates ReauthRequest |
| `/me` | GET | AuthenticatedUser | Returns user info from DB via user_repo::find_by_uuid() |

**Auth Service** (`backend/src/services/auth_service.rs`):

1. **`login()`**:
   - Finds user by username (returns generic error if not found)
   - Verifies password with `verify_password()`
   - On mismatch: logs security event "failed_login"
   - Generates JWT token via `generate_token()` with user id, uuid, username, role, department_id
   - Returns `LoginResponse` with token, token_type "Bearer", expires_in, user info

2. **`change_password()`**:
   - Requires ReauthRequired guard (15-min window)
   - Verifies current password
   - Validates new password complexity
   - Hashes and updates: `user_repo::update_password()`
   - Logs security event "password_changed"

3. **`reauth()`**:
   - Verifies password
   - Updates `last_reauth_at` timestamp
   - No side effects if password invalid (already logged at auth_guard level)

### 3. RESPONSE ENVELOPE

#### A. ApiResponse (`backend/src/utils/response.rs`)

**Main Response Wrapper:**
```rust
pub struct ApiResponse<T: Serialize> {
    pub success: bool,           // always true for 2xx, false for errors
    pub data: T,                 // the actual payload
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub message: Option<String>, // optional message
}
```

**Methods:**
- `ApiResponse::ok(data: T)` → returns `Json<Self>` with success=true, message=None
- `ApiResponse::ok_with_message(data: T, message)` → success=true with optional message

**Error Response:**
```rust
pub struct ApiError {
    pub status: u16,                                          // HTTP status code
    pub error: String,                                        // status reason (e.g., "Unauthorized")
    pub message: String,                                      // error message
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub details: Option<serde_json::Value>,                  // optional details
}
```

**Error Constructors:**
- `ApiError::new(status, message)`
- `ApiError::with_details(value)`
- `ApiError::bad_request(msg)` → 400
- `ApiError::unauthorized(msg)` → 401
- `ApiError::forbidden(msg)` → 403
- `ApiError::not_found(msg)` → 404
- `ApiError::internal(msg)` → 500

**AppError → HTTP Mapping:**
```rust
pub enum AppError {
    Database(sqlx::Error) → 500 internal
    Auth(String) → 401 unauthorized
    Validation(String) → 400 bad_request
    NotFound(String) → 404 not_found
    Forbidden(String) → 403 forbidden
    Internal(String) → 500 internal
}
```

**Catchers Registered** (in main.rs):
- 404 → `not_found()` → `ApiError::not_found("Resource not found")`
- 401 → `unauthorized()` → `ApiError::unauthorized("Authentication required")`
- 422 → `unprocessable()` → `ApiError::bad_request("Invalid request data")`
- 429 → `too_many_requests()` → `ApiError::new(Status::TooManyRequests, "Rate limit exceeded...")`
- 500 → `internal_error()` → `ApiError::internal("An internal error occurred")`

#### B. Health Route (`backend/src/routes/health.rs`)

**Response Structure:**
```rust
pub struct HealthResponse {
    pub status: String,  // "ok"
    pub service: String, // "campus-learn-backend"
}
```
- Endpoint: `GET /health`
- Returns plain `Json<HealthResponse>` (no ApiResponse wrapper)

#### C. Info Route (`backend/src/routes/info.rs`)

**Response Structure:**

```rust
pub struct InfoResponse {
    pub name: String,        // "CampusLearn Operations Suite"
    pub version: String,     // from CARGO_PKG_VERSION
    pub description: String, // "Enterprise-grade campus learning management and operations platform"
    pub api_version: String, // "v1"
}
```
- Endpoint: `GET /api/v1/info`
- Returns plain `Json<InfoResponse>` (no ApiResponse wrapper)

```rust

    pub data: Vec<T>,
    pub pagination: Pagination,
}

pub struct Pagination {

    pub per_page: u32,

    pub total_pages: u64,
}
```

### 4. AUDIT ROUTES + LOGGING

#### A. Audit Model (`backend/src/models/audit.rs`)

```rust
pub struct AuditLog {
    pub id: i64,
    pub uuid: String,
    pub user_id: Option<i64>,
    pub action: String,              // e.g., "booking.create", "booking.approve"
    pub entity_type: String,         // e.g., "booking"
    pub entity_id: Option<i64>,      // the booking's DB id
    pub old_values: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub new_values: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub ip_address: Option<String>,
    pub user_agent: Option<String>,
    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,
    pub retention_expires_at: Option<NaiveDateTime>, // 7 years
    pub created_at: NaiveDateTime,
}

pub struct SecurityEvent {
    pub id: i64,
    pub uuid: String,
    pub event_type: String,    // e.g., "failed_login", "password_changed", "booking_auto_restriction"
    pub severity: String,      // "info", "warning"
    pub user_id: Option<i64>,
    pub ip_address: Option<String>,
    pub description: String,
    pub metadata: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,
    pub created_at: NaiveDateTime,
}
```

#### B. Audit Repository (`backend/src/repositories/audit_repo.rs`)

**SQL Queries:**

1. **Create Audit Log:**
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO audit_logs (uuid, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, old_values, new_values, ip_address, user_agent, correlation_id, retention_expires_at)
   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 YEAR))
   ```
   - 7-year retention

2. **List Audit Logs** (with optional filtering):
   ```sql
   -- If entity_type AND entity_id:
   SELECT * FROM audit_logs WHERE entity_type = ? AND entity_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?

   -- If entity_type only:
   SELECT * FROM audit_logs WHERE entity_type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?

   -- Otherwise all:
   SELECT * FROM audit_logs ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?
   ```
   - Limit defaults to 100, max 1000

#### C. Audit Service (`backend/src/services/audit_service.rs`)

```rust
pub async fn list_audit_logs(
    pool: &MySqlPool, entity_type: Option<&str>, entity_id: Option<i64>, limit: Option<i64>,
) -> Result<Vec<AuditLog>, AppError>
```
- Enforces limit min 100, max 1000
- Delegates to audit_repo

#### D. Audit Routes (`backend/src/routes/audit.rs`)

**Endpoint:**

| Endpoint | Method | Guard | Handler |
|----------|--------|-------|---------|
| `/?entity_type=&entity_id=&limit=` | GET | AdminGuard | `list_audit_logs()` |

- Query params: entity_type (optional), entity_id (optional), limit (optional)
- Returns `Json<ApiResponse<Vec<AuditLog>>>`

---

### 5. ADDITIONAL MIDDLEWARE

#### A. CSRF Guard (`backend/src/middleware/csrf_guard.rs`)

**`CsrfOriginCheck` Fairing:**
- Logs warning if state-changing request (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) has mismatched Origin header
- Defense-in-depth; primary defense is Bearer token auth (not cookies)
- Allowed origin from env var `ALLOWED_ORIGIN` (default: "http://localhost:3000")
- Skips GET, HEAD, OPTIONS
- Allows requests without Origin header (non-browser, server-to-server)

#### B. Correlation ID (`backend/src/middleware/correlation.rs`)

**`CorrelationId` Fairing:**
- Extracts or generates `X-Correlation-Id` header per request
- Stored in request-local cache
- Used for tracing across logs and audit records
- Helper: `get_correlation_id(req)` retrieves the ID

---

### 6. RATE LIMIT REPOSITORY

#### C. Rate Limit Repo (`backend/src/repositories/rate_limit_repo.rs`)

**SQL Queries:**

1. **Get Request Count:**
   ```sql
   SELECT request_count FROM rate_limit_entries
   WHERE user_id = ? AND window_start > DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
   ORDER BY window_start DESC LIMIT 1
   ```

2. **Increment Request Count:**
   ```sql
   UPDATE rate_limit_entries SET request_count = request_count + 1
   WHERE user_id = ? AND window_start > DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
   
   -- If no rows affected:
   INSERT INTO rate_limit_entries (user_id, window_start, request_count)
   VALUES (?, NOW(), 1)
   ```

3. **Cleanup Old Entries:**
   ```sql
   DELETE FROM rate_limit_entries WHERE window_start < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 5 MINUTE)
   ```
   - Called hourly via background job (see main.rs)

content.text:
1	# Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: Fail
5	
6	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
7	- What was reviewed: `README.md`, `SELF_TEST_REPORT.md`, compose/proxy config, backend entry points, route registration, auth/security middleware, core services/repositories/models/migrations, frontend routes/pages/API client/CSS, and the `unit_tests/` plus `API_tests/` suites.
8	- What was not reviewed: runtime behavior, browser rendering, database state after execution, Docker orchestration success, TLS behavior in a live environment, background jobs actually running, webhook delivery reachability, and real test execution.
9	- What was intentionally not executed: the project, Docker, tests, builds, and external services.
10	- Claims requiring manual verification: actual TLS/proxy behavior, live booking concurrency under parallel requests, background-job cadence, webhook delivery/retry behavior, and real UI rendering across devices.
11	
12	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
13	- Prompt core goal: one offline portal covering course authoring/versioned publishing, two-step approvals, shared-resource booking, compliance/risk alerts, local auth/RBAC, sensitive-data handling, and offline-only integrations.
14	- Main implementation areas mapped: Rocket backend (`backend/src/main.rs:21`, `backend/src/routes/mod.rs:1`), Dioxus frontend (`frontend/src/main.rs:24`), MySQL schema/migrations (`backend/migrations/20240101000000_initial_schema.sql:8`), auth/security middleware (`backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:15`, `backend/src/middleware/reauth_guard.rs:17`, `backend/src/middleware/hmac_guard.rs:14`), and test suites (`unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:1`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:1`).
15	- Headline fit: the repository is a real full-stack codebase with relevant modules, but several prompt-critical behaviors are incomplete, weakly enforced, or only partially surfaced in the UI.
16	
17	## 4. Section-by-section Review
18	
19	### 1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
20	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
21	- Rationale: Startup/test/config instructions and entry points exist, but documentation materially overclaims runtime proof and contains static inconsistencies. The README says admins can trigger scheduled publishing, but the route actually requires HMAC verification; the repo defines HMAC tables but no HMAC key management or seed path, so the integration path is not statically verifiable end-to-end.
22	- Evidence: `README.md:38`, `README.md:145`, `README.md:180`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:77`, `backend/src/middleware/hmac_guard.rs:22`, `backend/migrations/20240102000000_phase2_schema.sql:14`, `backend/src/routes/mod.rs:1`, `SELF_TEST_REPORT.md:107`
23	- Manual verification note: actual startup and HMAC-key provisioning would require running the stack or manually populating DB state, which was intentionally not done.
24	
25	### 1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt
26	- Conclusion: Fail
27	- Rationale: The implementation is broadly aligned with the business domain, but it materially misses prompt-critical user flows: privacy export/deletion/rectification are placeholders, risk subscriptions/webhooks are not wired to event generation, booking approval visibility/scope is incomplete, and the frontend omits major required flows such as media handling, booking reviewer workflows, unpublish UI, and change-diff presentation.
28	- Evidence: `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:39`, `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:92`, `backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs:103`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:96`, `frontend/src/pages/course_editor.rs:117`, `frontend/src/pages/bookings.rs:109`, `frontend/src/pages/course_detail.rs:127`
29	
30	### 2.1 Whether the delivered project fully covers the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt
31	- Conclusion: Fail
32	- Rationale: Core requirements are only partially covered. Course version diffs are generated but not exposed as a user flow; booking approvers cannot view breaches and bookings are not department-scoped; privacy export/delete/rectify are not fully implemented; webhook subscriptions are stored but not delivered; and the frontend does not provide several required operational flows.
33	- Evidence: `backend/src/services/version_service.rs:24`, `backend/src/repositories/course_repo.rs:259`, `backend/src/routes/courses.rs:251`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:86`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:41`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:45`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:48`, `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:186`, `frontend/src/api/mod.rs:106`, `frontend/src/pages/bookings.rs:120`
34	
35	### 2.2 Whether the delivered project represents a basic end-to-end deliverable from 0 to 1
36	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
37	- Rationale: This is not a snippet-only repository; it has a complete project skeleton, docs, migrations, backend, frontend, and tests. However, several flows stop at placeholder persistence or partially wired UI, so some advertised end-to-end scenarios are not statically complete.
38	- Evidence: `README.md:255`, `backend/src/main.rs:96`, `frontend/src/main.rs:24`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:41`, `backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs:103`, `frontend/src/pages/privacy.rs:80`
39	
40	### 3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition
41	- Conclusion: Pass
42	- Rationale: The backend is clearly layered by routes/services/repositories/models, and the frontend is split into pages/components/layout/API/types. Core concerns are not piled into a single file.
43	- Evidence: `backend/src/main.rs:3`, `backend/src/routes/mod.rs:1`, `backend/src/services/mod.rs:1`, `backend/src/repositories/mod.rs:1`, `frontend/src/main.rs:1`, `frontend/src/layouts/mod.rs:6`
44	
45	### 3.2 Whether the project shows maintainability and extensibility
46	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
47	- Rationale: The module decomposition is maintainable, but several features are only half-wired, and the test suite relies heavily on Python mirrors of Rust logic rather than executing production code. That weakens change safety and makes future extension riskier.
48	- Evidence: `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:6`, `unit_tests/backend/test_version_diff.py:6`, `unit_tests/backend/test_scheduled_publish.py:6`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:360`
49	
50	### 4.1 Whether engineering details reflect professional software practice
51	- Conclusion: Fail
52	- Rationale: There is solid structure around tracing, audit logs, JWTs, and parameterized SQL, but important professional controls are incomplete: rate limiting only applies after authentication, re-auth is not enforced for all admin actions, booking approval lacks object scope, the API success envelope is inconsistent, and privacy input validation is incomplete on the sensitive-data endpoint.
53	- Evidence: `backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:39`, `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:16`, `API_tests/test_rate_limit.py:117`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:38`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:96`, `backend/src/routes/audit.rs:12`, `backend/src/utils/response.rs:5`, `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:91`, `backend/src/routes/privacy.rs:64`, `backend/src/dto/privacy.rs:29`
54	
55	### 4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service
56	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
57	- Rationale: The repo looks like a real product in structure, but some flows remain at the level of illustrative persistence instead of full product behavior, especially in privacy export/delete/rectify and risk-subscription delivery.
58	- Evidence: `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:39`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:48`, `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:186`, `backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs:131`
59	
60	### 5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal and constraints
61	- Conclusion: Fail
62	- Rationale: The codebase understands the domain, but key prompt semantics are weakened or ignored: two-step approvals can be bypassed by admin role semantics, department/term scoping does not cover booking approvals, privacy workflows are not materially complete, and the responsive console loses navigation on mobile.
63	- Evidence: `backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:77`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:169`, `backend/src/repositories/booking_repo.rs:145`, `backend/migrations/20240101000000_initial_schema.sql:237`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:39`, `frontend/assets/main.css:192`, `frontend/src/layouts/mod.rs:45`
64	
65	### 6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario and demonstrates reasonable visual quality
66	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
67	- Rationale: The frontend has a coherent design system, responsive CSS, tabs, tables, badges, toasts, modals, and status styling. However, responsive behavior is materially broken because the sidebar is hidden on mobile with no replacement navigation, and several required interaction flows are absent from the UI.
68	- Evidence: `frontend/assets/main.css:2`, `frontend/assets/main.css:63`, `frontend/assets/main.css:93`, `frontend/assets/main.css:191`, `frontend/src/layouts/mod.rs:45`, `frontend/src/pages/bookings.rs:109`, `frontend/src/pages/course_editor.rs:117`
69	- Manual verification note: visual polish, spacing, and render correctness still require human browser review.
70	
71	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
72	
73	### High
74	- Title: Privacy export/delete/rectify workflows are placeholder implementations
75	  - Conclusion: Fail
76	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:39`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:45`, `backend/src/services/privacy_service.rs:48`, `backend/src/repositories/privacy_repo.rs:79`
77	  - Impact: Export only records a synthetic file path, delete only removes `sensitive_data_vault` rows, and rectify immediately completes with no data change. The delivered privacy workflow does not materially satisfy offline export/deletion with administrator approval.
78	  - Minimum actionable fix: Implement real export generation, define and execute full-account deletion/retention rules, and add a real rectification workflow with field-level targets and auditability.
79	
80	- Title: Risk subscriptions and webhook delivery are stored but never triggered by risk events
81	  - Conclusion: Fail
82	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:92`, `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:115`, `backend/src/services/risk_service.rs:186`, `backend/src/services/webhook_service.rs:103`
83	  - Impact: Users can create subscriptions, but new risk events only notify hard-coded roles; the optional webhook feature is not functionally integrated, so queued-local delivery on unreachable endpoints is not actually available.
84	  - Minimum actionable fix: On event creation, resolve matching subscriptions, enqueue webhook deliveries, and deliver in-app notifications according to subscription settings instead of hard-coded role fan-out.
85	
86	- Title: Booking approval lacks department-scoped authorization and approver breach visibility
87	  - Conclusion: Fail
88	  - Evidence: `backend/migrations/20240101000000_initial_schema.sql:237`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:96`, `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs:327`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:86`, `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs:406`
89	  - Impact: The booking domain has no department field to scope approvals, any `ReviewerGuard` user can approve/reject pending bookings, and approvers cannot inspect breach history even though the prompt requires breach visibility for approvers.
90	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add department/ownership scope to resources or bookings, enforce it in approve/reject service logic, and add reviewer/admin breach views tied to booking decisions.
91	
92	- Title: Rate limiting and anti-scraping do not protect login or other unauthenticated entry points
93	  - Conclusion: Fail
94	  - Evidence: `backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:39`, `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:16`, `API_tests/test_rate_limit.py:117`
95	  - Impact: Brute-force login attempts and unauthenticated scraping are outside the implemented limiter, despite the prompt requiring rate limiting and basic anti-scraping controls.
96	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add IP and account-aware throttling for `/auth/login` and other unauthenticated high-value endpoints, and document/test those controls.
97	
98	- Title: Administrative re-authentication is only partially enforced
99	  - Conclusion: Fail
100	  - Evidence: `backend/src/routes/risk.rs:40`, `backend/src/routes/privacy.rs:51`, `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:42`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:38`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:96`, `backend/src/routes/audit.rs:12`
101	  - Impact: Some admin actions require recent re-auth, but others with clear administrative impact do not, including course approval decisions, booking approval/rejection, and audit access. This does not match the prompt's 15-minute re-auth requirement for administrative actions.
102	  - Minimum actionable fix: Define the admin-action set explicitly and apply `ReauthRequired` consistently to every qualifying route.
103	
104	- Title: Frontend omits multiple prompt-critical course release flows
105	  - Conclusion: Fail
106	  - Evidence: `backend/src/routes/courses.rs:190`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:87`, `frontend/src/api/mod.rs:106`, `frontend/src/pages/course_editor.rs:117`, `frontend/src/pages/course_detail.rs:127`
107	  - Impact: The backend exposes media and unpublish functionality, but the Dioxus UI only supports course details, sections, lessons, and submit-for-approval. There is no UI for media upload/validation, no tag-management flow, no unpublish flow, and no clear diff presentation between approved releases.
108	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add frontend media/tag/unpublish/version-diff flows and surface read-only prior-version content in the course detail/version UI.
109	
110	- Title: Frontend omits booking reviewer workflow and immediate conflict UX, and mobile navigation is broken
111	  - Conclusion: Fail
112	  - Evidence: `frontend/src/pages/bookings.rs:109`, `frontend/src/api/mod.rs:139`, `frontend/assets/main.css:192`, `frontend/src/layouts/mod.rs:45`
113	  - Impact: The booking page only supports the current user's bookings/resources/breaches, never calls availability for immediate feedback, has no reviewer approval queue, and hides the only navigation on mobile. This falls short of the prompt's responsive console requirement.
114	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add a reviewer booking-approval view, wire live availability/conflict checks into booking entry, and provide a mobile navigation alternative when the sidebar is hidden.
115	
116	- Title: Department/term data isolation is weakened by role-wide notification fan-out
117	  - Conclusion: Fail
118	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/notification_service.rs:18`, `backend/src/repositories/user_repo.rs:80`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:76`, `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs:161`
119	  - Impact: Course and booking notifications are broadcast to every user in a role, not to department-scoped reviewers. This leaks request metadata across departments and conflicts with the prompt's data-scope requirement.
120	  - Minimum actionable fix: Replace role-wide fan-out with department/term-scoped recipient resolution tied to the relevant entity.
121	
122	### Medium
123	- Title: Documentation and self-test reporting overstate what is statically verifiable and contain route mismatches
124	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
125	  - Evidence: `README.md:145`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:77`, `README.md:189`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:7`, `SELF_TEST_REPORT.md:107`
126	  - Impact: Reviewers are told that admins can trigger scheduled transitions and that the project has already been proven at runtime, but the route protection and static audit boundary do not support those claims. This reduces trust in the delivery package.
127	  - Minimum actionable fix: Align README and self-test claims with actual route guards, verification paths, and what is proven statically versus only manually.
128	
129	- Title: Success response contract is inconsistent with the documented API envelope
130	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
131	  - Evidence: `README.md:27`, `backend/src/utils/response.rs:5`, `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:91`, `backend/src/routes/info.rs:13`, `backend/src/routes/health.rs:11`
132	  - Impact: `message: null` is omitted from standard success responses, and `/auth/me`, `/info`, and `/health` bypass the common response type entirely. Client behavior and docs can drift.
133	  - Minimum actionable fix: Standardize all success endpoints on one documented envelope, or explicitly document exceptions.
134	
135	- Title: HMAC integration support is incomplete from a delivery-verification standpoint
136	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
137	  - Evidence: `backend/migrations/20240102000000_phase2_schema.sql:14`, `backend/src/middleware/hmac_guard.rs:74`, `backend/src/routes/mod.rs:1`, `backend/src/services/seed.rs:101`
138	  - Impact: The repo contains HMAC verification logic and schema, but no route or seed path provisions usable HMAC keys, leaving the integration path incomplete for a static reviewer.
139	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add documented key provisioning/rotation management and static instructions or migration seeds for safe local verification.
140	
141	- Title: Unit tests are mostly non-executing mirrors and sometimes diverge from the Rust implementation
142	  - Conclusion: Fail
143	  - Evidence: `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:6`, `unit_tests/backend/test_version_diff.py:6`, `unit_tests/backend/test_scheduled_publish.py:8`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:360`, `unit_tests/backend/test_api_response_shape.py:8`
144	  - Impact: These tests can pass while the real Rust code behaves differently. The scheduled-publish unit test already documents formats that the Rust service does not accept.
145	  - Minimum actionable fix: Add Rust unit tests or higher-value black-box API tests for critical rules, and remove or clearly label mirror tests that are not executable verification of production code.
146	
147	## 6. Security Review Summary
148	
149	- Authentication entry points: Partial Pass
150	  - Evidence: `backend/src/routes/auth.rs:16`, `backend/src/auth/jwt.rs:18`, `backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:22`
151	  - Reasoning: JWT-based auth and `/auth/me` guards are present, but login is not rate-limited and HMAC integration provisioning is incomplete.
152	
153	- Route-level authorization: Partial Pass
154	  - Evidence: `backend/src/routes/courses.rs:17`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:38`, `backend/src/routes/risk.rs:15`, `backend/src/routes/audit.rs:12`
155	  - Reasoning: Most routes use explicit guards, but re-auth is not applied consistently and some sensitive actions rely only on broad role guards.
156	
157	- Object-level authorization: Fail
158	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/course_service.rs:57`, `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs:327`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:145`
159	  - Reasoning: Course ownership checks are present, but booking approval/rejection lacks entity scope checks, and approval review does not enforce department/term scope at the mutation point.
160	
161	- Function-level authorization: Partial Pass
162	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:153`, `backend/src/middleware/auth_guard.rs:77`, `backend/src/services/booking_service.rs:257`
163	  - Reasoning: Self-approval prevention and ownership checks exist, but admin-bypass semantics and missing re-auth weaken higher-risk functions.
164	
165	- Tenant / user isolation: Partial Pass
166	  - Evidence: `backend/src/services/course_service.rs:27`, `backend/src/services/approval_service.rs:282`, `backend/src/services/notification_service.rs:18`, `backend/src/routes/bookings.rs:86`
167	  - Reasoning: Course and approval reads are partly scoped by department/term, but notifications are faned out by role only, and booking approvals/breach visibility do not implement comparable scoping.
168	
169	- Admin / internal / debug protection: Partial Pass
170	  - Evidence: `backend/src/routes/risk.rs:18`, `backend/src/routes/audit.rs:15`, `backend/src/routes/approvals.rs:80`, `docker-compose.override.yml:1`
171	  - Reasoning: Admin-only and HMAC-protected routes exist, but the internal scheduled-transition trigger is not statically operable end-to-end, and dev HTTP exposure remains documented in the delivery.
172	
173	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
174	
175	- Unit tests: Fail
176	  - Evidence: `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:6`, `unit_tests/backend/test_scheduled_publish.py:8`, `unit_tests/backend/test_api_response_shape.py:8`
177	  - Reasoning: The unit suite largely reimplements rules in Python instead of executing Rust code; some mirrors already diverge from the backend.
178	
179	- API / integration tests: Partial Pass
180	  - Evidence: `API_tests/test_auth.py:52`, `API_tests/test_courses.py:34`, `API_tests/test_approvals.py:34`, `API_tests/test_bookings.py:33`, `API_tests/test_security_regression.py:81`
181	  - Reasoning: Coverage breadth is good on paper, but several critical areas are not covered: HMAC/process-scheduled, webhook queueing/delivery, booking approval scope, full privacy export/delete semantics, and HTTPS/proxy behavior.
182	
183	- Logging categories / observability: Partial Pass
184	  - Evidence: `backend/src/main.rs:23`, `backend/src/repositories/audit_repo.rs:4`, `backend/src/repositories/security_repo.rs:4`, `backend/src/middleware/correlation.rs:1`
185	  - Reasoning: Structured tracing, audit logs, security events, and correlation IDs are present. This is a strength of the delivery.
186	
187	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses: Partial Pass
188	  - Evidence: `backend/src/models/user.rs:10`, `backend/src/services/auth_service.rs:29`, `backend/src/repositories/audit_repo.rs:14`, `backend/src/routes/notifications.rs:22`
189	  - Reasoning: Password hashes are not serialized and I found no direct plaintext SSN logging, but broad notification fan-out leaks operational metadata, and static analysis cannot fully prove runtime log hygiene.
190	
191	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
192	
193	### 8.1 Test Overview
194	- Unit tests exist under `unit_tests/` and use Python `unittest`: `run_tests.sh:19`, `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:1`, `unit_tests/frontend/test_route_definitions.py:1`.
195	- API / integration tests exist under `API_tests/` and also use Python `unittest`: `run_tests.sh:59`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:1`, `API_tests/test_security_regression.py:1`.
196	- Documentation provides test commands: `README.md:180`, `CLAUDE.md:13`, `run_tests.sh:16`.
197	- Important boundary: API tests default to direct HTTP backend access, not the HTTPS proxy path highlighted elsewhere: `README.md:94`, `README.md:191`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:7`.
198	
199	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
200	
201	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
202	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
203	| JWT login and authenticated `/me` | `API_tests/test_auth.py:53`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:84` | Successful token retrieval and `401` on missing/invalid token: `API_tests/test_auth.py:55`, `API_tests/test_auth.py:92` | Basically covered | Does not cover login throttling or proxy/TLS path | Add auth tests for login throttling, HTTPS proxy path, and token expiration behavior |
204	| Password complexity | `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:22` | Pure-Python mirror function: `unit_tests/backend/test_password.py:6` | Insufficient | Does not execute Rust validator; could drift silently | Add Rust unit tests for `validate_password_complexity` and API tests for change-password validation errors |
205	| Course ownership and draft visibility | `API_tests/test_courses.py:126`, `API_tests/test_courses.py:168`, `API_tests/test_scope_isolation.py:116` | Cross-author `403` and student draft denial: `API_tests/test_courses.py:222`, `API_tests/test_courses.py:291` | Basically covered | No test for version-diff exposure because no route exists | Add tests for version-diff endpoint once implemented and author/reviewer term-scope edge cases |
206	| Two-step approval happy path and rejection | `API_tests/test_approvals.py:34`, `API_tests/test_approvals.py:123`, `API_tests/test_approvals.py:254` | Step sequencing and self-approval block: `API_tests/test_approvals.py:68`, `API_tests/test_approvals.py:73` | Basically covered | No test that admin re-auth is required for approval decisions; no test for same-admin multi-step bypass | Add approval tests for re-auth enforcement and per-step actor separation |
207	| Booking creation, conflicts, reschedule, cancel | `API_tests/test_bookings.py:33`, `API_tests/test_booking_approval.py:33`, `unit_tests/backend/test_booking_rules.py:77` | Conflict and ownership assertions: `API_tests/test_bookings.py:75`, `API_tests/test_bookings.py:208` | Basically covered | No coverage for reviewer scope, approver breach visibility, or live availability UX | Add API tests for department-scoped booking approval and reviewer breach inspection |
208	| Re-auth enforcement on admin actions | `API_tests/test_auth.py:119`, `API_tests/test_privacy.py:133`, `API_tests/test_risk.py:165`, `API_tests/test_security_regression.py:14` | `403` before `/auth/reauth`, success after: `API_tests/test_privacy.py:145`, `API_tests/test_risk.py:175` | Insufficient | Approvals, booking approvals, and audit access are not tested for re-auth because the code does not enforce it | Add tests for every admin mutation/read classified as sensitive |
209	| Risk subscriptions and webhook support | `API_tests/test_risk.py:99`, `unit_tests/backend/test_webhook_endpoint_validation.py:217` | Only creation/validation paths are asserted: `API_tests/test_risk.py:115`, `unit_tests/backend/test_webhook_endpoint_validation.py:220` | Missing | No test that a risk event enqueues webhook deliveries or honors subscriptions | Add API/black-box tests that create subscriptions, generate events, and verify queue rows / notification delivery |
210	| Privacy export/delete/rectify behavior | `API_tests/test_privacy.py:32`, `API_tests/test_privacy.py:76` | Only status `200` on request/review: `API_tests/test_privacy.py:46`, `API_tests/test_privacy.py:100` | Insufficient | No assertion that export content exists, deletion removes all personal data, or rectification changes data | Add tests for export artifact contents, complete deletion semantics, and rectification target updates |
211	| HMAC anti-replay and scheduled transitions endpoint | None covering live route | N/A | Missing | No tests for `X-HMAC-*` validation, nonce replay, or `/approvals/process-scheduled` | Add API tests for valid signature, replay rejection, expired timestamps, and scheduled transition execution |
212	| API response envelope consistency | `unit_tests/backend/test_api_response_shape.py:5` | Static dictionary assertions only: `unit_tests/backend/test_api_response_shape.py:8` | Insufficient | Does not hit live handlers; real routes already diverge from the documented envelope | Add API tests that assert actual JSON envelopes for representative success and error routes |
213	| HTTPS / proxy verification path | None | N/A | Missing | API tests use `http://localhost:8000` by default | Add proxy-path tests against `https://localhost` for health, auth, and a protected route |
214	
215	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
216	- Authentication: Basically covered by `API_tests/test_auth.py:52`, but severe login-bruteforce defects could still remain undetected because login throttling is not tested and not implemented.
217	- Route authorization: Partially covered by `API_tests/test_courses.py:78`, `API_tests/test_risk.py:74`, and `API_tests/test_audit.py:56`, but several sensitive admin endpoints are untested for re-auth because they are not guarded.
218	- Object-level authorization: Partially covered for course ownership in `API_tests/test_courses.py:168`, but severe defects can still remain in booking approval scope because no test exercises department-scoped reviewer boundaries.
219	- Tenant / data isolation: Partially covered for course/approval reads in `API_tests/test_scope_isolation.py:75` and `API_tests/test_security_regression.py:287`, but notification fan-out and booking reviewer scope are effectively untested.
220	- Admin / internal protection: Insufficient. There are tests for some admin-only routes, but none for HMAC-protected internal operations or end-to-end integration-key handling.
221	
222	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
223	- Fail
224	- Major risks covered: basic auth happy path, course ownership checks, draft visibility, two-step approval happy path, booking conflict basics, and some re-auth checks.
225	- Major uncovered risks: login throttling, HMAC/internal endpoints, webhook delivery, full privacy export/delete/rectify semantics, booking reviewer scope, department-scoped notification leakage, HTTPS/proxy behavior, and actual Rust unit-level verification.
226	- Bottom line: the tests could still pass while severe authorization and delivery defects remain.
227	
228	## 9. Final Notes
229	- This audit stayed within the static-only boundary and does not claim runtime success.
230	- The repository has credible architecture and relevant domain modules, but the current delivery is not acceptable against the prompt because multiple high-severity gaps remain in security boundaries, privacy workflows, subscription/webhook delivery, and prompt-critical UI coverage.
231
